titre: Actions are judged by their intentions
contenu: On the authority of the Commander of the Faithful, Abu Hafs 'Umar ibn Al-Khattab (may Allah be pleased with him), who said: I heard the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) say: 'Actions are but by their intentions, and every person will have only what they intended. So whoever emigrated for the sake of Allah and His Messenger, his emigration was for Allah and His Messenger. And whoever emigrated to gain some worldly benefit or to take a woman in marriage, then his emigration was for that for which he emigrated.' (Hadith No. 1/42, 40 Hadith Nawawi, Reported by Al-Bukhari in his Sahih No. 1)

On the authority of 'Umar ibn Al-Khattab (may Allah be pleased with him) also, who said: One day while we were sitting with the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ), there came before us a man with very white clothing and very black hair. There were no signs of travel on him, and none of us knew him. He sat down in front of the Prophet (ﷺ), placed his knees against the Prophet's knees, put his hands on his thighs, and said: 'O Muhammad, inform me about Islam.' The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: 'Islam is to testify that there is no deity worthy of worship but Allah and that Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah, to establish the prayer, to give Zakah, to fast in Ramadan, and to make the pilgrimage to the House if you are able to do so.' He said: 'You have spoken truthfully.' We were amazed at him asking and then confirming it. He then said: 'Inform me about Iman.' He (ﷺ) said: 'It is to believe in Allah, His Angels, His Books, His Messengers, the Last Day, and to believe in Divine Decree — both its good and its evil.' He said: 'You have spoken truthfully. Now inform me about Ihsan.' He (ﷺ) said: 'It is that you worship Allah as if you see Him, for even though you cannot see Him, He surely sees you.' He said: 'Now inform me about the Hour.' He (ﷺ) said: 'The one questioned about it knows no more than the one questioning.' He said: 'Then inform me about its signs.' He (ﷺ) said: 'That the slave-girl will give birth to her mistress, and that you will see barefooted, naked, destitute shepherds competing in constructing tall buildings.' Then he left. I stayed for a long time, and then the Prophet (ﷺ) asked: 'O Umar, do you know who the questioner was?' I said: 'Allah and His Messenger know best.' He said: 'It was Jibreel, who came to teach you your religion.' (Hadith No. 2/42, 40 Hadith Nawawi, Reported by Muslim in his Sahih)

On the authority of Abu 'Abd Al-Rahman 'Abdullah ibn 'Umar ibn Al-Khattab (may Allah be pleased with both of them), who said: I heard the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) say: 'Islam is built upon five pillars: testifying that there is no deity worthy of worship but Allah and that Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah, establishing the prayer, giving Zakah, making the pilgrimage to the House, and fasting in Ramadan.' (Hadith No. 3/42, 40 Hadith Nawawi, Reported by Al-Bukhari and Muslim)

titre: Creation of Man and His Destiny
contenu: On the authority of Abu 'Abd Al-Rahman 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him), who said: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ), the truthful and believed, told us: 'Verily the creation of each of you is gathered in his mother's womb for forty days as a drop of fluid, then it becomes a clinging clot for a similar period, then a lump of flesh for a similar period. Then an angel is sent to him who breathes into him the soul and is ordered to write down four things: his provision, his lifespan, his deeds, and whether he will be among the wretched or the blessed. By Allah, besides Whom there is no deity worthy of worship, one of you may perform the deeds of the people of Paradise until there is only a cubit between him and it, and then what has been decreed overtakes him and he performs the deeds of the people of Hellfire and enters it. And one of you may perform the deeds of the people of Hellfire until there is only a cubit between him and it, and then what has been decreed overtakes him and he performs the deeds of the people of Paradise and enters it.' (Hadith No. 4/42, 40 Hadith Nawawi, Reported by Al-Bukhari and Muslim)

On the authority of the Mother of the Believers, 'A'ishah Umm Abdillah (may Allah be pleased with her), who said that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: 'Whoever introduces into this matter of ours something that is not part of it, it will be rejected.' In another version narrated by Muslim: 'Whoever does an action that is not in accordance with this matter of ours, it will be rejected.' (Hadith No. 5/42, 40 Hadith Nawawi, Reported by Al-Bukhari and Muslim)

On the authority of Abu 'Abdullah Al-Nu'man ibn Bashir (may Allah be pleased with both of them), who said: I heard the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) say: 'Verily, the lawful is clear and the unlawful is clear, and between them are matters that are ambiguous, which many people do not know. Whoever guards himself against the ambiguous matters has protected his religion and his honor. And whoever falls into the ambiguous matters falls into that which is unlawful, just as a shepherd who grazes his flock around a protected sanctuary will eventually graze in it. Verily, every king has a protected sanctuary, and the sanctuary of Allah on earth is what He has made unlawful. Verily within the body there is a morsel of flesh; if it is sound, the entire body is sound, and if it is corrupt, the entire body is corrupt. Truly, it is the heart.' (Hadith No. 6/42, 40 Hadith Nawawi, Reported by Al-Bukhari and Muslim)

On the authority of Abu Ruqayyah Tamim ibn Aws Al-Dari (may Allah be pleased with him), the Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Religion is sincere advice (nasiha).' We said: 'For whom?' He said: 'For Allah, for His Book, for His Messenger, and for the leaders of the Muslims and their common people.' (Hadith No. 7/42, 40 Hadith Nawawi, Reported by Muslim)

On the authority of Ibn 'Umar (may Allah be pleased with both of them), that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: 'I have been commanded to fight the people until they testify that there is no deity worthy of worship but Allah and that Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah, and they establish the prayer and give the Zakah. If they do that, their blood and wealth are protected from me except by the right of Islam, and their reckoning will be with Allah.' (Hadith No. 8/42, 40 Hadith Nawawi, Reported by Al-Bukhari and Muslim)

On the authority of Abu Hurairah 'Abd Al-Rahman ibn Sakhr (may Allah be pleased with him), who said: I heard the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) say: 'Avoid that which I have forbidden you, and do that which I have commanded you to the best of your ability. Verily, what has destroyed those before you was the multitude of their questions and their disagreement with their prophets.' (Hadith No. 9/42, 40 Hadith Nawawi, Reported by Al-Bukhari and Muslim)

titre: Allah Accepts Only What is Pure
contenu: On the authority of Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him), who said: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: 'Verily Allah is pure and accepts only that which is pure. Allah has commanded the believers to do what He has commanded the Messengers, and He says: "O Messengers! Eat of the pure things, and do righteous deeds." [Al-Mu'minun: 51] And He says: "O you who believe! Eat of the pure things We have provided for you." [Al-Baqarah: 172] Then he mentioned a man who travels extensively, his hair disheveled and covered with dust, extending his hands toward the sky saying: O Lord! O Lord! — yet his food is unlawful, his drink is unlawful, his clothing is unlawful, and he has been nourished by that which is unlawful. How then could his supplication be accepted?' (Hadith No. 10/42, 40 Hadith Nawawi, Reported by Muslim)

On the authority of Abu Muhammad Al-Hasan ibn 'Ali ibn Abi Talib (may Allah be pleased with both of them), the grandson of the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) and his beloved, who said: I memorized from the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ): 'Leave that which makes you doubt for that which does not make you doubt.' (Hadith No. 11/42, 40 Hadith Nawawi, Reported by Al-Tirmidhi No. 2520 and Al-Nasa'i; Al-Tirmidhi said it is Hasan Sahih)

titre: Leaving What Does Not Concern You
contenu: On the authority of Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him), who said: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: 'A sign of a person's good observance of Islam is that he leaves alone that which does not concern him.' (Hadith No. 12/42, 40 Hadith Nawawi, A Hasan hadith, reported by Al-Tirmidhi No. 2318 and Ibn Majah No. 3976)

titre: Love for Your Brother What You Love for Yourself
contenu: On the authority of Abu Hamzah Anas ibn Malik (may Allah be pleased with him), the servant of the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ), from the Prophet (ﷺ), who said: 'None of you truly believes until he loves for his brother what he loves for himself.' (Hadith No. 13/42, 40 Hadith Nawawi, Reported by Al-Bukhari No. 13 and Muslim No. 45)

On the authority of Ibn Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him), who said: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: 'It is not permissible to shed the blood of a Muslim who testifies that there is no deity worthy of worship but Allah and that I am the Messenger of Allah, except in one of three cases: the married person who commits adultery, a life taken in retaliation for a life, and the one who forsakes his religion and separates from the community.' (Hadith No. 14/42, 40 Hadith Nawawi, Reported by Al-Bukhari and Muslim)

On the authority of Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him), that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: 'Whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day, let him speak good or remain silent. Whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day, let him be generous to his neighbor. And whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day, let him be generous to his guest.' (Hadith No. 15/42, 40 Hadith Nawawi, Reported by Al-Bukhari and Muslim)

On the authority of Abu Ya'la Shaddad ibn Aws (may Allah be pleased with him), that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: 'Verily Allah has decreed excellence (ihsan) in all things. So when you kill, do so in a good manner; and when you slaughter, do so in a good manner. Let each of you sharpen his blade and spare his slaughtered animal any suffering.' (Hadith No. 17/42, 40 Hadith Nawawi, Reported by Muslim)

On the authority of Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him), that a man said to the Prophet (ﷺ): 'Counsel me.' He said: 'Do not get angry.' The man repeated his request several times, and each time the Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Do not get angry.' (Hadith No. 16/42, 40 Hadith Nawawi, Reported by Al-Bukhari)

On the authority of Abu Dharr Jundub ibn Junadah and Abu 'Abd Al-Rahman Mu'adh ibn Jabal (may Allah be pleased with both of them), from the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ), who said: 'Fear Allah wherever you are, follow a bad deed with a good deed and it will wipe it out, and treat people with good character.' (Hadith No. 18/42, 40 Hadith Nawawi, A Hasan hadith reported by Al-Tirmidhi No. 1987)

On the authority of Abu Al-'Abbas 'Abdullah ibn 'Abbas (may Allah be pleased with both of them), who said: I was riding behind the Prophet (ﷺ) one day when he said: 'O young man, I will teach you some words: Be mindful of Allah and He will protect you. Be mindful of Allah and you will find Him in front of you. If you ask, ask only of Allah. If you seek help, seek it only from Allah. Know that if the entire nation were to gather to benefit you with something, they would only benefit you with something that Allah had already written for you, and if they gathered to harm you with something, they would only harm you with something Allah had already written against you. The pens have been lifted and the pages have dried.' (Hadith No. 19/42, 40 Hadith Nawawi, Reported by Al-Tirmidhi No. 2516 who said it is Hasan Sahih) In another version: 'Be mindful of Allah, you will find Him before you. Recognize Allah in times of ease and He will recognize you in times of difficulty. Know that what has missed you was never going to befall you, and what has befallen you was never going to miss you. Know that victory comes with patience, relief comes after hardship, and with difficulty comes ease.'

On the authority of Abu Mas'ud 'Uqbah ibn 'Amr Al-Ansari Al-Badri (may Allah be pleased with him), who said: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: 'Among the words preserved from the earlier prophets is: If you have no shame (hayaa), then do as you wish.' (Hadith No. 20/42, 40 Hadith Nawawi, Reported by Al-Bukhari)

titre: Say I Believe in Allah, Then Be Steadfast
contenu: On the authority of Abu 'Amr — or Abu 'Amrah — Sufyan ibn 'Abdullah (may Allah be pleased with him), who said: I said: 'O Messenger of Allah, tell me a thing about Islam which I can ask of no one but you.' He said: 'Say: I believe in Allah, then be steadfast.' (Hadith No. 21/42, 40 Hadith Nawawi, Reported by Muslim)

On the authority of Abu 'Abdullah Jabir ibn 'Abdullah Al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with both of them), that a man asked the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ): 'Do you think that if I perform the obligatory prayers, fast in Ramadan, treat the lawful as lawful, and the unlawful as unlawful, and do nothing further, I will enter Paradise?' He said: 'Yes.' (Hadith No. 22/42, 40 Hadith Nawawi, Reported by Muslim)

On the authority of Abu Malik Al-Harith ibn 'Asim Al-Ash'ari (may Allah be pleased with him), who said: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: 'Purity is half of faith. Alhamdulillah fills the scale. SubhanAllah and Alhamdulillah fill what is between the heavens and the earth. Prayer is a light; charity is a proof; patience is illumination; and the Quran is an argument for or against you. Every person begins his morning trading his soul — either he frees it or ruins it.' (Hadith No. 23/42, 40 Hadith Nawawi, Reported by Muslim)

On the authority of Abu Dharr Al-Ghifari (may Allah be pleased with him), from the Prophet (ﷺ), among what he related from his Lord (Exalted be He), that He said: 'O My servants! I have made oppression unlawful for Myself and I have made it unlawful among you, so do not oppress one another. O My servants, all of you are astray except those whom I guide, so seek guidance from Me and I will guide you. O My servants, all of you are hungry except those whom I feed, so ask sustenance from Me and I will feed you. O My servants, all of you are naked except those whom I clothe, so ask clothing from Me and I will clothe you. O My servants, you sin by night and by day, and I forgive all sins, so seek forgiveness from Me and I will forgive you. O My servants, you will never attain the power to do Me harm, nor will you attain the power to benefit Me. O My servants, if the first of you and the last of you, the humans and the jinn, were all as pious as the most pious heart of any single man, that would not increase My Kingdom in the slightest. O My servants, if the first and the last of you, the humans and the jinn, were all as wicked as the most wicked heart of any single man, that would not decrease My Kingdom in the slightest. O My servants, if the first and the last of you, the humans and the jinn, were all to stand in one place and make a request of Me, and I gave everyone what they requested, that would not decrease what I have by even what a needle takes from the sea. O My servants, it is only your deeds that I account for you, and then recompense you for. So let whoever finds good praise Allah, and let whoever finds other than that blame no one but himself.' (Hadith No. 24/42, 40 Hadith Nawawi, Reported by Muslim)

titre: Every Good Deed is a Charity
contenu: On the authority of Abu Dharr (may Allah be pleased with him) also: Some of the companions of the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said to the Prophet (ﷺ): 'O Messenger of Allah, the affluent have taken all the rewards; they pray as we pray, they fast as we fast, and they give charity from their surplus wealth.' He said: 'Has Allah not made things for you to give as charity? Every SubhanAllah is charity, every Alhamdulillah is charity, every Allahu Akbar is charity, every La ilaha illallah is charity, commanding what is good is charity, forbidding what is evil is charity, and the sexual act you perform with your wife is charity.' They said: 'O Messenger of Allah, would one of us fulfill his desire and still receive a reward?' He said: 'If he were to fulfill it in a forbidden way, would he not bear a sin? Likewise, if he fulfills it in a lawful way, he will receive a reward.' (Hadith No. 25/42, 40 Hadith Nawawi, Reported by Muslim)

On the authority of Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him), who said: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: 'Upon every joint of a person there is a charitable obligation every day the sun rises: to judge equitably between two people is a charity; to help a man with his mount, lifting him onto it or hoisting his luggage onto it, is a charity; a good word is charity; every step you take toward the prayer is charity; and removing a harmful thing from the road is charity.' (Hadith No. 26/42, 40 Hadith Nawawi, Reported by Al-Bukhari and Muslim)

Al-Nawwas ibn Sam'an (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Piety (al-birr) is good character, and sin is that which wavers in your soul and which you dislike people finding out about.' (Hadith No. 27/42, 40 Hadith Nawawi, Reported by Muslim) And Wabisah ibn Ma'bad (may Allah be pleased with him) said: I came to the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) and he said: 'You have come to ask about piety?' I said: 'Yes.' He said: 'Consult your heart. Piety is that which makes the soul tranquil and the heart at peace, and sin is that which unsettles the soul and causes uncertainty in the chest, even if people give you their legal opinion in its favor and continue to do so.' A Hasan hadith reported in the Musnad of Ahmad and in the Sunan of Al-Darimi with a good chain of narrators.

On the authority of Abu Najih Al-'Irbad ibn Sariyah (may Allah be pleased with him), who said: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) gave us a sermon which made our hearts fearful and our eyes tearful. We said: 'O Messenger of Allah, it is as if this is a farewell sermon, so give us your instructions.' He said: 'I enjoin you to have taqwa (fear) of Allah, and to listen and obey even if an Abyssinian slave rules over you. Whoever among you lives after me will see many differences. So adhere to my Sunnah and the Sunnah of the Rightly-Guided Caliphs; hold fast to it and bite onto it with your molars. Beware of newly-invented matters, for every newly-invented matter is an innovation, and every innovation is going astray.' (Hadith No. 28/42, 40 Hadith Nawawi, Reported by Abu Dawud and Al-Tirmidhi No. 266)

titre: The Gates of Goodness and the Paths of Guidance
contenu: On the authority of Mu'adh ibn Jabal (may Allah be pleased with him), who said: I said: 'O Messenger of Allah, tell me about an act which will allow me to enter Paradise and keep me away from the Fire.' He said: 'You have asked about a great matter, yet it is easy for one for whom Allah makes it easy. Worship Allah and do not associate anything with Him, establish the prayer, give the Zakah, fast in Ramadan, and perform the pilgrimage to the House.' Then he said: 'Shall I not guide you to the gates of goodness? Fasting is a shield; charity extinguishes sins as water extinguishes fire; and a man's prayer in the middle of the night.' Then he recited: 'Who forsake their beds, invoking their Lord in fear and hope, and who spend out of what We have bestowed on them.' [Al-Sajdah: 16-17] Then he said: 'Shall I not tell you of the head of the matter, its pillar, and its pinnacle?' I said: 'Yes, O Messenger of Allah.' He said: 'The head of the matter is Islam, its pillar is prayer, and its pinnacle is jihad in the way of Allah.' Then he said: 'Shall I not tell you the key to all of that?' I said: 'Yes, O Messenger of Allah.' He took hold of his tongue and said: 'Restrain this.' I said: 'O Prophet of Allah, will we be held responsible for what we say?' He said: 'May your mother be bereaved of you, O Mu'adh! Is there anything that casts people headlong into the Hellfire other than the harvests of their tongues?' (Hadith No. 29/42, 40 Hadith Nawawi, Reported by Al-Tirmidhi No. 2616)

On the authority of Abu Tha'labah Al-Khushani Jurthum ibn Nashir (may Allah be pleased with him), from the Prophet (ﷺ), who said: 'Verily, Allah has set obligations, so do not neglect them; He has set limits, so do not transgress them; He has prohibited things, so do not violate them; and He has remained silent about other matters out of mercy for you, not out of forgetfulness, so do not inquire into them.' (Hadith No. 30/42, 40 Hadith Nawawi, A Hasan hadith reported by Al-Daraqutni and others; considered weak by some scholars)

titre: The Love of Allah and of People
contenu: On the authority of Abu Al-'Abbas Sahl ibn Sa'd Al-Sa'idi (may Allah be pleased with him), who said: A man came to the Prophet (ﷺ) and said: 'O Messenger of Allah, show me a deed which, if I do it, will cause Allah to love me and people to love me.' He said: 'Renounce the world and Allah will love you, and renounce what people have and they will love you.' (Hadith No. 31/42, 40 Hadith Nawawi, A Hasan hadith reported by Ibn Majah No. 4102; considered weak by some scholars)

On the authority of Abu Sa'id Sa'd ibn Malik ibn Sinan Al-Khudri (may Allah be pleased with him), that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: 'There should be neither harming nor reciprocating harm.' (Hadith No. 32/42, 40 Hadith Nawawi, A Hasan hadith reported by Ibn Majah No. 2341, Al-Daraqutni and others)

On the authority of Ibn 'Abbas (may Allah be pleased with both of them), that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: 'If people were given everything they claimed, men would claim the wealth and blood of others. The burden of proof is upon the claimant, and the taking of an oath is upon the one who denies.' (Hadith No. 33/42, 40 Hadith Nawawi, A Hasan hadith reported by Al-Bayhaqi and others; part of it is in the two Sahihs)

titre: Forbidding Evil is a Religious Duty
contenu: On the authority of Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri (may Allah be pleased with him), who said: I heard the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) say: 'Whoever among you sees an evil, let him change it with his hand. If he is unable, then with his tongue. If he is unable, then with his heart — and that is the weakest of faith.' (Hadith No. 34/42, 40 Hadith Nawawi, Reported by Muslim)

Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: 'Do not envy one another, do not outbid one another to raise the price, do not hate one another, do not turn away from one another, and do not undersell one another. Be, O servants of Allah, brothers. A Muslim is the brother of a Muslim: he does not wrong him, he does not forsake him, he does not lie to him, and he does not belittle him. Taqwa is here' — and he pointed to his chest three times. 'It is enough of an evil for a man to look down upon his Muslim brother. All of a Muslim is inviolable to another Muslim: his blood, his wealth, and his honor.' (Hadith No. 35/42, 40 Hadith Nawawi, Reported by Muslim)

On the authority of Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him), that the Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Whoever relieves a believer of a hardship from the hardships of this world, Allah will relieve him of a hardship from the hardships of the Day of Resurrection. Whoever makes things easy for someone in difficulty, Allah will make things easy for him in this world and in the Hereafter. Whoever covers the faults of a Muslim, Allah will cover his faults in this world and in the Hereafter. Allah continues to help a servant as long as the servant is helping his brother. Whoever treads a path in search of knowledge, Allah will make easy for him a path to Paradise. No people gather together in one of the houses of Allah, reciting the Book of Allah and studying it together, without tranquility descending upon them, mercy enveloping them, the angels surrounding them, and Allah mentioning them to those in His presence. Whoever is slowed down by his deeds will not be hastened forward by his lineage.' (Hadith No. 36/42, 40 Hadith Nawawi, Reported by Muslim)

titre: The Reward of Good Deeds is Multiplied
contenu: On the authority of Ibn 'Abbas (may Allah be pleased with both of them), from the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ), among what he related from his Lord (Exalted be He): 'Verily, Allah has recorded good deeds and evil deeds and then clarified them. Whoever resolves to do a good deed but does not do it, Allah records it as one complete good deed. If he resolves to do it and does it, Allah records it as ten to seven hundred times its worth, or even more. Whoever resolves to do an evil deed but does not do it, Allah records it as one complete good deed. If he resolves to do it and does it, Allah records it as only one evil deed.' (Hadith No. 37/42, 40 Hadith Nawawi, Reported by Al-Bukhari and Muslim)

titre: How to Attain the Love of Allah
contenu: On the authority of Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him), who said: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: 'Allah (Exalted be He) said: Whoever shows enmity to a friend of Mine, I declare war on him. My servant does not draw near to Me with anything more loved by Me than the religious duties I have made obligatory upon him. My servant continues to draw near to Me with supererogatory works until I love him. When I love him, I am his hearing with which he hears, his sight with which he sees, his hand with which he strikes, and his foot with which he walks. Were he to ask of Me, I would surely give him; and were he to seek refuge in Me, I would surely grant him refuge.' (Hadith No. 38/42, 40 Hadith Nawawi, Reported by Al-Bukhari)

On the authority of Ibn 'Abbas (may Allah be pleased with both of them), that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: 'Truly, Allah has forgiven my nation for mistakes, forgetfulness, and that which they are compelled to do.' (Hadith No. 39/42, 40 Hadith Nawawi, A Hasan hadith reported by Ibn Majah No. 2045, Al-Bayhaqi in Sunan and others)

titre: Be in This World as a Stranger
contenu: On the authority of Ibn 'Umar (may Allah be pleased with both of them), who said: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) took hold of my shoulder and said: 'Be in this world as though you were a stranger or a traveler passing through.' Ibn 'Umar used to say: 'If you reach the evening, do not wait for the morning, and if you reach the morning, do not wait for the evening. Take from your health for your illness and from your life for your death.' (Hadith No. 40/42, 40 Hadith Nawawi, Reported by Al-Bukhari)

On the authority of Abu Muhammad 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr ibn Al-'As (may Allah be pleased with both of them), who said that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: 'None of you truly believes until his desires are subservient to what I have brought.' (Hadith No. 41/42, 40 Hadith Nawawi, A Hasan Sahih hadith reported by Al-Asbahani in Kitab al-Hujjah; considered weak by some scholars)

On the authority of Anas (may Allah be pleased with him), who said: I heard the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) say: 'Allah (Exalted be He) said: O son of Adam, as long as you call upon Me and put your hope in Me, I have forgiven you for what you have done and I do not mind. O son of Adam, even if your sins were to reach the clouds of the sky and you then asked forgiveness of Me, I would forgive you. O son of Adam, if you were to come to Me with sins nearly as great as the earth and you then faced Me, associating nothing with Me, I would bring you forgiveness nearly as great as it.' (Hadith No. 42/42, 40 Hadith Nawawi, Reported by Al-Tirmidhi No. 3540)
